title: The Prayer Wall
subtitle: Write It Down. Let It Go.
introduction: The walls of the Prayer Space are crafted from Douglas fir with slender gaps between each piece. You’re invited to write a prayer on one of the small pieces of paper provided, fold it up, and tuck it into the wall. Your prayer doesn’t stay hidden—others in the community will read it and pray over it. That’s the point. Your prayers are carried by the people of this place.
wallInstruction: Grab a piece of paper and a pen from the table. Write whatever is on your heart. Fold it up and tuck it into the wall. That’s it. There’s no formula. Others will find it, read it, and pray for you. If you’re not sure what to write, the prayer guides below can help you get started.
guidesIntro: These short guides are here to help if you feel stuck or just want a little direction. Pick one that fits where you are today.

guides.title: When You Need to Say Thank You
guides.description: Sometimes we rush past the good stuff. This guide helps you slow down and notice what God has given you today.
steps: Take a breath. Get quiet for a second.
Think about the last 24 hours. What’s one thing—big or small—that you’re thankful for?
Write it down. Be specific. Not just “my family” but maybe “the way my kid laughed at dinner last night.”
Tell God thank you. Mean it. Fold it up and tuck it in the wall—someone else will find it and thank God with you.
guides.scripture: Give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.

guides.title: When You’re Carrying Something Heavy
guides.description: You don’t have to carry it alone. This guide helps you name what’s weighing on you and hand it to God.
steps: Close your eyes for a moment. What’s the first thing that comes to mind when you think about what’s been heavy lately?
Write it down honestly. You don’t have to be polished or spiritual. Just be real.
Read it back to yourself. Then say to God: “I’m giving this to You.”
Fold it up and tuck it into the wall. You’re not forgetting it—you’re releasing it. And someone else will carry it with you in prayer.
guides.scripture: Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you.

guides.title: When Someone Else Needs Prayer
guides.description: Maybe you came in thinking about someone who’s struggling. This guide helps you pray for them.
steps: Who’s on your mind right now? Picture them.
What do they need? It’s okay if you’re not totally sure. God knows.
Write their name and what you’re asking God to do for them.
Fold it up and tuck it in the wall. Someone else will find it and pray for them too.
guides.scripture: Bear one another’s burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.

guides.title: When You Need to Get Honest
guides.description: This isn’t about guilt. It’s about freedom. Sometimes the bravest prayer is the most honest one.
steps: Ask God to bring to mind anything you’ve been avoiding or holding onto.
Write it down. You don’t have to explain yourself. Just name it.
Ask God for forgiveness, or for the strength to forgive someone else.
Fold it up and tuck it in the wall. Leave it there. Others will pray over it—you don’t have to carry it alone.
guides.scripture: If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just and will forgive us our sins and purify us from all unrighteousness.

guides.title: When You Don’t Know What’s Next
guides.description: If you’re in a season of uncertainty—waiting, deciding, wondering—this guide helps you bring that to God.
steps: What’s the decision or situation you keep turning over in your mind?
Write it down. Be honest about what you’re feeling—confused, anxious, hopeful, stuck.
Ask God: “What do You want me to see that I’m not seeing?”
Fold it up and tuck it in the wall. You don’t need the answer right now. Someone else will find this and ask God on your behalf.
guides.scripture: Trust in the Lord with all your heart, and do not lean on your own understanding. In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make straight your paths.

guides.title: When You Don’t Have Words
guides.description: You don’t have to say anything profound. Sometimes the best prayer is just showing up.
steps: Sit down. Take a few slow breaths.
You don’t have to write anything yet. Just be here.
If something comes to mind—a word, a name, a feeling—write that down. Even one word counts.
If nothing comes, that’s okay too. Your presence here is a prayer. Tuck whatever you’ve got into the wall—even one word is enough for someone else to pray over.
guides.scripture: Be still, and know that I am God.
